title: Contributing to resmush
text: outlines propose change resmush. detailed discussion contributing tidyverse packages, please see development contributing guide code review principles.

title: Contributing to resmush
text: can fix typos, spelling mistakes, grammatical errors documentation directly using GitHub web interface, long changes made source file. generally means ’ll need edit roxygen2 comments .R, .Rd file. can find .R file generates .Rd reading comment first line.

title: Contributing to resmush
text: want make bigger change, ’s good idea first file issue make sure someone team agrees ’s needed. ’ve found bug, please file issue illustrates bug minimal reprex (also help write unit test, needed). See guide create great issue advice.

title: Contributing to resmush
text: Fork package clone onto computer. haven’t done , recommend using usethis::create_from_github("dieghernan/resmush", fork = TRUE). Install development dependencies devtools::install_dev_deps(), make sure package passes R CMD check running devtools::check(). R CMD check doesn’t pass cleanly, ’s good idea ask help continuing. Create Git branch pull request (PR). recommend using usethis::pr_init("brief-description--change"). Make changes, commit git, create PR running usethis::pr_push(), following prompts browser. title PR briefly describe change. body PR contain Fixes #issue-number. user-facing changes, add bullet top NEWS.md (.e. just first header). Follow style described https://style.tidyverse.org/news.html.

title: Contributing to resmush
text: New code follow tidyverse style guide. can use Air apply style, please don’t restyle code nothing PR. use roxygen2, Markdown syntax, documentation. use testthat unit tests. Contributions test cases included easier accept.

title: Contributing to resmush
text: contributing guide adapted tidyverse contributing guide available https://tidyverse.tidyverse.org/CONTRIBUTING.html.

title: Using resmush
text: Compress online jpg image resmush_url(): () (b) Figure 1: Original image Figure 1 (): 178.7 KB, optimized image Figure 1 (b): 45 KB (compression: 74.8%). Click enlarge. Use qlty argument adjust compression quality jpg files. Keep value 90 maintain good image quality.  Figure 2: Image visible compression artifacts caused high compression (qlty = 3), compared Figure 1 (b). optimization functions invisibly return data frame summarizes process. following example shows output compressing local file:
code: library(resmush)  url <- "https://dieghernan.github.io/resmush/img/jpg_example_original.jpg"  resmush_url(url, outfile = "jpg_example_compress.jpg", overwrite = TRUE) #> ══ resmush summary ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ #> ℹ Input: 1 URL, total size 178.7 Kb. #> ✔ Optimized 1 URL: Size is now 45 Kb (was 178.7 Kb). Saved 133.7 Kb (74.82%). #> Saved result in directory '.'. # Use an extreme compression setting. resmush_url(   url,   outfile = tempfile(fileext = ".jpg"),   overwrite = TRUE,   qlty = 3 ) #> ══ resmush summary ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ #> ℹ Input: 1 URL, total size 178.7 Kb. #> ✔ Optimized 1 URL: Size is now 2.2 Kb (was 178.7 Kb). Saved 176.4 Kb (98.74%). #> Saved result in directory 'tempdir()'. png_file <- system.file("extimg/example.png", package = "resmush")  # Copy to a temporary file for this example. tmp_png <- tempfile(fileext = ".png") file.copy(png_file, tmp_png, overwrite = TRUE) #> [1] TRUE  summary <- resmush_file(tmp_png, overwrite = TRUE)  tibble::as_tibble(summary[, -c(1, 2)]) #> # A tibble: 1 × 6 #>   src_size dest_size compress_ratio notes src_bytes dest_bytes #>   <chr>    <chr>     <chr>          <chr>     <dbl>      <dbl> #> 1 239.9 Kb 70.7 Kb   70.54%         OK       245618      72356

title: Using resmush
text: Several R packages also provide image optimization tools: xfun::tinify(): Similar resmush_file() uses TinyPNG requires API key. xfun::optipng(): Compresses local files using OptiPNG, must installed locally. tinieR package jmablog: R interface TinyPNG. tinyimg package (Xie 2026): Optimizes local png jpg/jpeg files using Rust libraries. optout package @coolbutuseless: Similar xfun::optipng() options. Requires additional local software. Table 1: R packages: comparison image optimization alternatives. Table 2: R packages: supported formats.

code: @Manual{R-resmush,   title = {{resmush}: Optimize and Compress Image Files with {reSmush.it}},   doi = {10.32614/CRAN.package.resmush},   author = {Diego Hernangómez},   year = {2026},   version = {1.0.1},   url = {https://dieghernan.github.io/resmush/},   abstract = {Optimize and compress local image files, directories and online images with the reSmush.it API <https://resmush.it/>. Supports png, jpg/jpeg, gif, bmp and tiff files.}, }

what: resmush | Optimize and compress images with reSmush.it
title: resmush | Optimize and compress images with reSmush.it
text: resmush R package optimizing compressing images reSmush.. reSmush.free API image optimization available WordPress many tools. reSmush.includes: Free image optimization API key required. Support local online images. Support png, jpg/jpeg, gif, bmp tiff files. Maximum image size: 5 MB. PNGQuant: Removes unnecessary chunks png files preserving full alpha transparency. JPEGOptim: Lossless optimization based Huffman table optimization. OptiPNG: png optimizer used several online compression tools.

title: resmush | Optimize and compress images with reSmush.it
text: Install resmush CRAN :

title: resmush | Optimize and compress images with reSmush.it
text: Compress online jpg image resmush_url(): () (b) Figure 1: Original image (): 178.7 KB, optimized image (b): 45 KB (compression: 74.8%). Click enlarge. Use qlty argument adjust compression quality jpg files. Keep value 90 maintain good image quality.  Figure 2: Image visible compression artifacts caused high compression (qlty = 3). optimization functions invisibly return data frame summarizes process. following example shows output compressing local file:
code: library(resmush)  url <- "https://dieghernan.github.io/resmush/img/jpg_example_original.jpg"  resmush_url(   url,   outfile = "man/figures/jpg_example_compress.jpg",   overwrite = TRUE ) #> ══ resmush summary ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ #> ℹ Input: 1 URL, total size 178.7 Kb. #> ✔ Optimized 1 URL: Size is now 45 Kb (was 178.7 Kb). Saved 133.7 Kb (74.82%). #> Saved result in directory 'man/figures'. # Use an extreme compression setting. resmush_url(   url,   outfile = "man/figures/jpg_example_compress_low.jpg",   overwrite = TRUE,   qlty = 3 ) #> ══ resmush summary ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ #> ℹ Input: 1 URL, total size 178.7 Kb. #> ✔ Optimized 1 URL: Size is now 2.2 Kb (was 178.7 Kb). Saved 176.4 Kb (98.74%). #> Saved result in directory 'man/figures'. png_file <- system.file("extimg/example.png", package = "resmush")  # Copy to a temporary file for this example. tmp_png <- tempfile(fileext = ".png") file.copy(png_file, tmp_png, overwrite = TRUE) #> [1] TRUE  summary <- resmush_file(tmp_png, overwrite = TRUE)  tibble::as_tibble(summary[, -c(1, 2)]) #> # A tibble: 1 × 6 #>   src_size dest_size compress_ratio notes src_bytes dest_bytes #>   <chr>    <chr>     <chr>          <chr>     <dbl>      <dbl> #> 1 239.9 Kb 70.7 Kb   70.54%         OK       245618      72356

what: Clean files created by resmush — resmush_clean_dir
title: Clean files created by resmush — resmush_clean_dir
text: Use caution. removes files computer. Clean one directories files created resmush_file().

title: Clean files created by resmush — resmush_clean_dir
Clean files created by resmush — resmush_clean_dir
text: dir character vector full path names. See path argument list.files(). suffix Character. Defaults "_resmush". See resmush_file(). recursive Logical. file search recurse directories?

title: Clean files created by resmush — resmush_clean_dir
text: Returns invisible() NULL value produces messages summarizing process.

title: Clean files created by resmush — resmush_clean_dir
code: # \donttest{ # Simple example.  png_file <- system.file("extimg/example.png", package = "resmush")  # Copy to a temporary file with a given suffix. suffix <- "_would_be_removed" tmp_png <- file.path(   tempdir(),   paste0("example", suffix, ".png") )  file.exists(tmp_png) #> [1] FALSE file.copy(png_file, tmp_png, overwrite = TRUE) #> [1] TRUE  file.exists(tmp_png) #> [1] TRUE  # Run with the default suffix. This should not remove the file. resmush_clean_dir(tempdir()) #> ℹ No files to clean in /tmp/Rtmp1Aglb0 with suffix "_resmush".  file.exists(tmp_png) #> [1] TRUE  # Use the matching suffix to remove the file. resmush_clean_dir(tempdir(), suffix = suffix) #> ℹ Removing 1 file: #> → /tmp/Rtmp1Aglb0/example_would_be_removed.png  file.exists(tmp_png) #> [1] FALSE # }

what: Optimize image files in directories — resmush_dir
title: Optimize image files in directories — resmush_dir
text: Optimize supported image files one directories reSmush.API.

title: Optimize image files in directories — resmush_dir
code: resmush_dir(   dir,   ext = "\\.(png|jpe?g|bmp|gif|tif)$",   suffix = "_resmush",   overwrite = FALSE,   progress = TRUE,   report = TRUE,   recursive = FALSE,   ... )

title: Optimize image files in directories — resmush_dir
text: dir character vector paths local directories. ext regex indicating extensions files optimize. default captures extensions supported API. suffix Character. Defaults "_resmush". default, optimized files saved directory suffix. example, example.png becomes example_resmush.png. Values "", NA NULL equivalent overwrite = TRUE. overwrite Logical. files dir overwritten? TRUE suffix ignored. progress Logical. progress bar displayed? report Logical. summary report displayed console? recursive Logical. file search within dir recursive? See also list.files(). ... Arguments passed resmush_file qlty Integer 0 100 indicating optimization level. affects jpg files. optimal results, use values 90. exif_preserve Logical. Exif metadata preserved? default FALSE, removes .

title: Optimize image files in directories — resmush_dir
text: Writes optimized files disk API call successful. Invisibly returns data frame summarizing process.

title: Optimize image files in directories — resmush_dir
code: # \donttest{ # Copy the example directory. example_dir <- system.file("extimg", package = "resmush") temp_dir <- tempdir() file.copy(example_dir, temp_dir, recursive = TRUE) #> [1] TRUE  # Create the destination folder path. dest_folder <- file.path(tempdir(), "extimg")  # Non-recursive. resmush_dir(dest_folder) #> ℹ Optimizing 2 files. #> ●∙∙ Go! | ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■□□□□□□□□□□□□□□□   50% [3ms] | ETA:  0s (1/2 files) #> ●∙∙ Go! | ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■  100% [1.9s] | ETA:  0s (2/2 files) #>  #> ══ resmush summary ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ #> ℹ Input: 2 files, total size 340.2 Kb. #> ✔ Optimized 2 files: Size is now 153.8 Kb (was 340.2 Kb). Saved 186.4 Kb (54.79%). #> Saved results in directory /tmp/Rtmp1Aglb0/extimg. resmush_clean_dir(dest_folder) #> ℹ Removing 2 files: #> → /tmp/Rtmp1Aglb0/extimg/example_resmush.jpg #> → /tmp/Rtmp1Aglb0/extimg/example_resmush.png  # Recursive. summary <- resmush_dir(dest_folder, recursive = TRUE) #> ℹ Optimizing 5 files. #> ●∙∙ Go! | ■■■■■■■□□□□□□□□□□□□□□□□□□□□□□□□   20% [1ms] | ETA:  0s (1/5 files) #> ∙●∙ Go! | ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■□□□□□□□□□□□□   60% [2.6s] | ETA:  2s (3/5 files) #> ∙●∙ Go! | ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■  100% [3.7s] | ETA:  0s (5/5 files) #>  #> ══ resmush summary ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ #> ℹ Input: 5 files, total size 401.7 Kb. #> ✔ Optimized 5 files: Size is now 173.5 Kb (was 401.7 Kb). Saved 228.2 Kb (56.81%). #> Saved results in directories /tmp/Rtmp1Aglb0/extimg, #> /tmp/Rtmp1Aglb0/extimg/top1/nested, /tmp/Rtmp1Aglb0/extimg/top1, and #> /tmp/Rtmp1Aglb0/extimg/top2.  # Return the same information in the invisible data frame. summary[, -c(1, 2)] #>   src_size dest_size compress_ratio notes src_bytes dest_bytes #> 1 100.4 Kb   83.2 Kb         17.15%    OK    102796      85164 #> 2 239.9 Kb   70.7 Kb         70.54%    OK    245618      72356 #> 3  17.8 Kb      6 Kb         66.48%    OK     18214       6105 #> 4  25.9 Kb    7.7 Kb         70.09%    OK     26499       7926 #> 5  17.8 Kb      6 Kb         66.48%    OK     18214       6105  # Display the `png` output. if (require("png", quietly = TRUE)) {   a_png <- grepl("png$", summary$dest_img)   my_png <- png::readPNG(summary[a_png, ]$dest_img[2])   grid::grid.raster(my_png) }   # Clean up the example files. unlink(dest_folder, force = TRUE, recursive = TRUE) # }

title: Optimize local image files — resmush_file
text: Optimize one local image files reSmush.API.

title: Optimize local image files — resmush_file
code: resmush_file(   file,   suffix = "_resmush",   overwrite = FALSE,   progress = TRUE,   report = TRUE,   qlty = 92,   exif_preserve = FALSE )

title: Optimize local image files — resmush_file
text: file Path paths local image files. API can optimize png, jpg/jpeg, gif, bmp tiff files. suffix Character. Defaults "_resmush". default, optimized file saved directory file suffix. example, example.png becomes example_resmush.png. Values "", NA NULL equivalent overwrite = TRUE. overwrite Logical. file file overwritten? TRUE suffix ignored. progress Logical. progress bar displayed? report Logical. summary report displayed console? qlty Integer 0 100 indicating optimization level. affects jpg files. optimal results, use values 90. exif_preserve Logical. Exif metadata preserved? default FALSE, removes .

title: Optimize local image files — resmush_file
text: Writes optimized files disk API call successful. Invisibly returns data frame summarizing process. report = TRUE, summary report also displayed console.

title: Optimize local image files — resmush_file
code: # \donttest{ png_file <- system.file("extimg/example.png", package = "resmush")  # Copy to a temporary file for this example. tmp_png <- tempfile(fileext = ".png")  file.copy(png_file, tmp_png, overwrite = TRUE) #> [1] TRUE  resmush_file(tmp_png) #> ══ resmush summary ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ #> ℹ Input: 1 file, total size 239.9 Kb. #> ✔ Optimized 1 file: Size is now 70.7 Kb (was 239.9 Kb). Saved 169.2 Kb (70.54%). #> Saved result in directory /tmp/Rtmp1Aglb0.  # Several paths. jpg_file <- system.file("extimg/example.jpg", package = "resmush") tmp_jpg <- tempfile(fileext = ".jpg")  file.copy(jpg_file, tmp_jpg, overwrite = TRUE) #> [1] TRUE  # Display a summary in the console. summary <- resmush_file(c(tmp_png, tmp_jpg)) #> ●∙∙ Go! | ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■□□□□□□□□□□□□□□□   50% [5ms] | ETA:  0s (1/2 files) #> ●∙∙ Go! | ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■  100% [1.1s] | ETA:  0s (2/2 files) #>  #> ══ resmush summary ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ #> ℹ Input: 2 files, total size 340.2 Kb. #> ✔ Optimized 2 files: Size is now 153.8 Kb (was 340.2 Kb). Saved 186.4 Kb (54.79%). #> Saved results in directory /tmp/Rtmp1Aglb0.  # The invisible data frame contains the same information. summary #>                                src_img #> 1 /tmp/Rtmp1Aglb0/file1c385c673ace.png #> 2 /tmp/Rtmp1Aglb0/file1c3815ec34bf.jpg #>                                       dest_img src_size dest_size #> 1 /tmp/Rtmp1Aglb0/file1c385c673ace_resmush.png 239.9 Kb   70.7 Kb #> 2 /tmp/Rtmp1Aglb0/file1c3815ec34bf_resmush.jpg 100.4 Kb   83.2 Kb #>   compress_ratio notes src_bytes dest_bytes #> 1         70.54%    OK    245618      72356 #> 2         17.15%    OK    102796      85164  # Display the `png` output. if (require("png", quietly = TRUE)) {   my_png <- png::readPNG(summary$dest_img[1])   grid::grid.raster(my_png) }   # Use with `jpg` files and parameters. resmush_file(tmp_jpg) #> ══ resmush summary ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ #> ℹ Input: 1 file, total size 100.4 Kb. #> ✔ Optimized 1 file: Size is now 83.2 Kb (was 100.4 Kb). Saved 17.2 Kb (17.15%). #> Saved result in directory /tmp/Rtmp1Aglb0. resmush_file(tmp_jpg, qlty = 10) #> ══ resmush summary ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ #> ℹ Input: 1 file, total size 100.4 Kb. #> ✔ Optimized 1 file: Size is now 6.4 Kb (was 100.4 Kb). Saved 94 Kb (93.61%). #> Saved result in directory /tmp/Rtmp1Aglb0. # }

title: Optimize online image files — resmush_url
text: Optimize download one online image files reSmush.API.

title: Optimize online image files — resmush_url
code: resmush_url(   url,   outfile = file.path(tempdir(), basename(url)),   overwrite = FALSE,   progress = TRUE,   report = TRUE,   qlty = 92,   exif_preserve = FALSE )

title: Optimize online image files — resmush_url
text: url URL vector URLs pointing hosted image files. API can optimize png, jpg/jpeg, gif, bmp tiff files. outfile Path paths optimized files stored disk. default, temporary files created tempfile() basename() file provided url. outfile must length url. overwrite Logical. outfile overwritten already exists? FALSE outfile exists, copy created numerical suffix, <outfile>_01.png. progress Logical. progress bar displayed? report Logical. summary report displayed console? qlty Integer 0 100 indicating optimization level. affects jpg files. optimal results, use values 90. exif_preserve Logical. Exif metadata preserved? default FALSE, removes .

title: Optimize online image files — resmush_url
text: Writes optimized files disk API call successful. Invisibly returns data frame summarizing process. value outfile duplicated, resmush_url() renames outputs suffixes _01 _02.

title: Optimize online image files — resmush_url
code: # \donttest{ # Base URL. base_url <- "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/dieghernan/resmush/main/inst/"  png_url <- paste0(base_url, "/extimg/example.png") resmush_url(png_url) #> ══ resmush summary ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ #> ℹ Input: 1 URL, total size 239.9 Kb. #> ✔ Optimized 1 URL: Size is now 70.7 Kb (was 239.9 Kb). Saved 169.2 Kb (70.54%). #> Saved result in directory /tmp/Rtmp1Aglb0.  # Several URLs. jpg_url <- paste0(base_url, "/extimg/example.jpg")  summary <- resmush_url(c(png_url, jpg_url)) #> ●∙∙ Go! | ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■□□□□□□□□□□□□□□□   50% [1ms] | ETA:  0s (1/2 URLs) #> ●∙∙ Go! | ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■  100% [1.1s] | ETA:  0s (2/2 URLs) #>  #> ══ resmush summary ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ #> ℹ Input: 2 URLs, total size 340.2 Kb. #> ✔ Optimized 2 URLs: Size is now 153.8 Kb (was 340.2 Kb). Saved 186.4 Kb (54.79%). #> Saved results in directory /tmp/Rtmp1Aglb0.  # The invisible data frame contains a summary of the process. summary #>                                                                              src_img #> 1 https://raw.githubusercontent.com/dieghernan/resmush/main/inst//extimg/example.png #> 2 https://raw.githubusercontent.com/dieghernan/resmush/main/inst//extimg/example.jpg #>                         dest_img src_size dest_size compress_ratio notes #> 1 /tmp/Rtmp1Aglb0/example_01.png 239.9 Kb   70.7 Kb         70.54%    OK #> 2    /tmp/Rtmp1Aglb0/example.jpg 100.4 Kb   83.2 Kb         17.15%    OK #>   src_bytes dest_bytes #> 1    245618      72356 #> 2    102796      85164  # Display the `png` output. if (require("png", quietly = TRUE)) {   my_png <- png::readPNG(summary$dest_img[1])   grid::grid.raster(my_png) }   # Use with `jpg` files and parameters. resmush_url(jpg_url) #> ══ resmush summary ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ #> ℹ Input: 1 URL, total size 100.4 Kb. #> ✔ Optimized 1 URL: Size is now 83.2 Kb (was 100.4 Kb). Saved 17.2 Kb (17.15%). #> Saved result in directory /tmp/Rtmp1Aglb0. resmush_url(jpg_url, qlty = 10) #> ══ resmush summary ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ #> ℹ Input: 1 URL, total size 100.4 Kb. #> ✔ Optimized 1 URL: Size is now 6.4 Kb (was 100.4 Kb). Saved 94 Kb (93.61%). #> Saved result in directory /tmp/Rtmp1Aglb0. # }

title: resmush 1.0.1
text: CRAN release: 2026-05-29 Improved console messages optimization reports cleanup helpers. Refactored internal code updated function documentation AI assistance.

title: resmush 1.0.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-03-14 Corrected typos. Migrated documentation Quarto. Minimum R version required: 4.1.0. Updated major version indicate package reached mature development state.

title: resmush 0.2.2
text: CRAN release: 2026-01-12 API calls now perform dry run ensure compressed file exists. API calls now include httr2::req_headers(referer = "https://dieghernan.github.io/resmush/").

title: resmush 0.2.1
text: CRAN release: 2024-12-18 Updated documentation.

title: resmush 0.2.0
text: CRAN release: 2024-07-19 Removed webp support API longer supports format.

title: resmush 0.1.1
text: CRAN release: 2024-05-22 Updated documentation.

title: resmush 0.1.0
text: CRAN release: 2024-02-02 Accepted CRAN. Added DOI: https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.10556679. Added png grid Suggests examples. Switched httr package httr2 package. resmush_clean_dir() now shows summary message. resmush_dir(), resmush_file() resmush_url() gained overwrite argument. resmush_dir(), resmush_file() resmush_url() gained progress report arguments, add new messages progress bars using cli::cli_progress_bar(). resmush_dir(), resmush_file() resmush_url() deprecated verbose argument.

title: resmush 0.0.1
text: Initial version.
